_note: Per-problem clinical triage. Authored strictly from each root cause's existing differentiators plus standard, well-established red flags. Weights point answers at root_cause ids that must exist on the problem. Expert-extendable; parse.js folds this onto graph.problems[].assessment.

knee-pain.intro: Four quick taps — this points you to the most likely cause, the way a physiotherapist would triage it.

options.label: Deep, or across the whole joint
Going down stairs, squatting, or after sitting a while
Stiffness first thing in the morning or after resting

questions.q: How did it come on, and your age?

options.label: Gradually, from training or overuse — I'm under 40
Slowly over months, and I'm 40 or older

redFlags.q: Before we go on — any of these?
redFlags.sub: Tap all that apply. These are the signs that need an in-person exam, not a self-care plan.
flags: The knee locks, catches, or suddenly gives way
I can't put weight on it, or can't fully straighten it
It's hot, red, or swollen — or the pain wakes me at night
It started right after a significant injury, fall, or twist
redFlags.message: What you've described — locking or giving way, being unable to bear weight, a hot or swollen joint, night pain, or a recent significant injury — should be checked in person before you start any self-care plan. Please see a doctor or physiotherapist first. You can still read the protocol below for background, but get assessed.

knee-pain.notMedicalAdvice: This is an educational self-check, not a diagnosis. If in doubt, see a clinician.
